solutions.solution: d. I is just the opposite of what the statement is saying. Even II is implicit. Evidence in industry and poor quality of life for workers, implies participatory management is safe.

a. None is implicit. The author asks him to consult 'me' before taking a decision. Obviously, he is worried that a decision without his consultation may not be correct. So, I is implicit. Further, he wouldn't have said unless he thought that taking a correct decision was important. Hence, II is implicit.

d. None is implicit. Assumption I is a mere twisted form of the given statement. Smoking is injurious. It means that non-smoking is not injurious, that's it. It doesn't mean that non-smoking promotes health. II is obvious to just the opposite of what it is true. Public warnings are given only when they are assumed to be necessary.

a. Children must be considering their teachers as role models, otherwise there is no reason why they would be influenced by them. Hence, I is implicit. II is not directly stated. It may or may not be true.

c. Both are implicit. The statement recognises the entertainment value of television and, at the same time, it asserts that its educational value 'cannot be ignored'. The tone of the passage is slightly lamenting which means that both the author wants to happen, is not happening presently. This means that both I and II are implicit.
a. When mother gives warning to the child, she must be assuming that the warning would be successful. II is not relevant (action can't make the mother.

b. The statement says that a car is required on rent. It does not say any vehicle is required. So, the statement cannot be said to have assumed that any vehicle is available for rent. But II is obviously implicit; almost an advertisement is made, it is assumed that people will respond to it. Company A wouldn't have given the advertisements.
b. I is definitely not mentioned in the advertisement. If is implicit, one couldn't have given the advertisements.

c. Unless the sale of all radio sets manufactured in India was known, the statement could not have been made. Hence, I is implicit. II is not implicit because we do not know about manufacturing. We know only about sales. X brand has the largest sale but it may not be the largest manufacturer of radio sets. Maybe company Y manufactures more sets but X does it better. In that case, Y is a bigger manufacturer while X exports all its sets. In that case, Y is a bigger manufacturer than X, would be going to Japan; A would not have advised him this. Maybe A is giving advice to B because B is A's friend.
c. Unless B would be lesser than that of X. Hence, I is implicit. But it is not certain that A gives advice to everybody.
b. Films have become indispensable but it does not imply that they are the only medium of entertainment. But it is certain that people enjoy films. Hence, II is implicit.

a. Leaning out of a running bus must be dangerous, otherwise the warning would not have been there. Hence, I is implicit. But II is not implicit. If the authorities would have assumed that children do not pay any heed to such warnings, they would not have put it up there.

b. II is not implicit. The company wants mechanical engineers. One reason could be that the company expects mechanical engineers to be good performers, as I suggests. But there could be another reason; for example, the company's supervisory job could be such that only a mechanical engineer could perform it. But one thing is certain, the advertisement was for supervisors; this means supervisors are needed. Hence, II is implicit.

d. The statement asks a man to be humble even after being victorious. This implies that people are usually not humble after victory. I is just the opposite of it. II is not implicit because it generalises the statement. Generally, people may be humble; the point is if they are humble or not after victory.

c. If the letter mentions expenses to be borne by candidates, those who sent the letter must have assumed that candidates may demand for reimbursement if the point is not clarified to them. Also, the candidates would not demand reimbursement if it was not a prevalent practice. So, I and II both are implicit.

d. To dislodge a corrupt government has been mentioned as the present purpose for the call of unity. But this does not mean that this is the only purpose. So, I is not implicit. Further, the leader asks like-minded parties to unite against the government and not the entire opposition. So, we cannot generalise that (all) opposition parties are non-corrupt. Hence, II is not implicit.

b. Postal rates have been increased the cause being to meet the deficit. This never means that the present rates are low. If the price of goods increases, it is not necessary that the earlier price was low. But the tone of the statement clearly implies that postal rates have been increased out of compulsion. So, II is implicit.

a. The statement says that if degrees are delinked from jobs, boys will not join colleges. This implies that jobs are a major reason for them to join college. So, I is implicit. Now, II is implicit, II is not because It is just the opposite of I.

a. The statement uses a tone that implies that if education is in a shambles, then the country deteriorates. This means that a good education is needed for the well-being of a nation. But this does not mean that a good education alone is sufficient for it. So, I is implicit but II is not.
